repro: Package A raised GL1 routine->high. schemas.ambiguity_blockers(carried-over GL1 test-author handoff, 'GL1-scaffold', 'high') returns 10 blockers because BLOCKING_AMBIGUITY_PROFILES=('high','critical') and the handoff carries 10 spec_ambiguities. Under v1/routine these were advisory (routine is not a blocking profile); under v2/high the H9 gate fires and would park GL1 before the implementer. This gate was NOT in the operator's pre-resolved set (only the GL1-floor-collision was pre-cleared).
recommendation: Proceed-as-read: the test-author's handoff documents (41 tests + 11 mutations) that the held-out corpus was deliberately built to accept BOTH readings of every one of the 10 ambiguities (each note states 'tests accept both' / 'tests pin the behavior, not the literal choice'). 'validated-wrong software' — the risk H9 exists to prevent — is therefore not present for these 10: whichever reading the implementer picks, the corpus passes. proceed-as-read is validated-safe by construction.

options.label: Adjudicate all 10 proceed-as-read; build GL1 now (corpus dual-safe)
Park GL1 on the 10 blockers; H9-on-scaffold-under-v2 becomes the pilot finding
Spawn a fresh test-author to shrink spec_ambiguities, then re-run H9

resolved.detail: Operator adjudicated proceed-as-read for all 10 GL1 spec ambiguities. Rationale: the carried-over test-author corpus is dual-safe by construction, so proceed-as-read carries no validated-wrong risk. Preserves v2 build-intent (firing advances past scaffold). H9-activation-by-Package-A recorded as pilot #3-v2 finding P3v2-1.
